title: Gap-Time AFT / Recurrent Survival Specialist
summary: This is a recurrent time-to-event problem with censoring at race end, not just independent binary rows.
rationale: This is a recurrent time-to-event problem with censoring at race end, not just independent binary rows. Official XGBoost AFT support and recurrent survival papers give a concrete way to model laps-until-next-stop and convert that into next-lap hazard.
implementation_hint: Create a target for laps until next pit within each Driver x Race, right-censor rows that reach the finish without another stop, and fit an XGBoost `survival:aft` model or a small recurrent survival specialist. Convert predicted survival to next-lap stop probability and blend it with the main classifier instead of replacing it.
expected_effect: Uses more structure than the raw binary label and should smooth noisy late-race probabilities while exploiting censored non-stop tails.
risk: AFT distribution choice can be wrong, and hazard conversion near the tail can be unstable without clipping or smoothing.
